    Oh Quantum! My Quantum!

    A space of infinite possibility.

    Philip A. LeMaitre

    Research Interests: Quantum Machine Learning, Quantum Foundations, Artificial Intelligence, Quantum Gravity, Relativistic Quantum Information.

    Welcome to my personal website and blog, dear Qubitzers! Take your shoes off and stay a while, why don’t you.

    Disclaimer: the views expressed on the site are my own and do not necessarily reflect the views of my colleagues, employers, affiliations, or anyone else mentioned on the site.

    Pinned Updates

    Major updates and useful entry points that deserve more than a one-off mention.

    See all updates

    Pinned update

    New paper and talk cycle

    The site now highlights work around relativistic quantum computing, contextuality harvesting, and conference material in a more navigable way.

    Research overview Latest blog posts

    Conference material

    Slides, posters, and videos are in one place

    Recent conference talks and posters are linked directly from the research page, so the site now works better as a central hub for talks and related material.

    Conference talks Posters

    Fresh paths through the site

    Research notes, outreach stories, and personal updates worth reading.

    Find what you are looking for quickly and in style.

    Search the site Find posts, talks, notes, and resources. Browse the blog by topic Jump into category, tag, and year landing pages from the blog index. Follow via RSS Subscribe in any feed reader.

    Featured Work

    Papers, talks, posters, and code I am particularly proud of

    See the full research page

    Papers

    Selected Papers

    See all papers

    Paper

    Harvesting Contextuality from the Vacuum

    Shows that detectors coupled to a quantum field can extract contextuality directly from the vacuum, extending the resource-harvesting program beyond entanglement.

    Paper

    A Universal Quantum Computer from Relativistic Motion

    Builds a quantum-computing architecture where gate operations arise from relativistic motion and field-mediated interactions.

    Paper

    Multi-Excitation Projective Simulation with a Many-Body Physics Inspired Inductive Bias

    Introduces a more expressive and interpretable learning architecture for projective simulation using a many-body inspired inductive bias.

    Talks and Posters

    Selected Talks and Posters

    See all talks

    Talk

    A Universal Quantum Computer From Relativistic Motion

    Recorded conference talk from the 15th Relativistic Quantum Information North Conference.

    Poster

    A Universal Quantum Computer from Relativistic Motion

    Poster version prepared for QISS 2025.

    Codes

    Selected Code Repositories

    See all code

    Code

    RVQC

    Code connected to the relativistic variational quantum computer work.

    Code

    Contextuality-Harvesting

    Simulation code for contextuality harvesting from quantum fields.

    Code

    quantum-chem

    Codebase underlying the polymer self-consistent field theory atom papers.

    What I hate is ignorance, smallness of imagination, the eye that sees no farther than its own lashes. All things are possible. Who you are is limited by who you think you are.

    — Egyptian Book of the Dead
